summary: A Taylor series approximates a function near a point by a polynomial built from its derivatives. Near x = 0 a few terms can stand in for the whole function.
method: Pick the variable and the point to expand around (the CAS tab uses x = 0).
Compute the function's value and successive derivatives at that point.
Build the polynomial: the coefficient of xⁿ is the nth derivative at the point divided by n!.
Keep terms up to the chosen order; the more terms, the wider the accurate range.

example.title: Series of eˣ around 0
given: expansion point x = 0, up to x³
steps: Every derivative of eˣ is eˣ, which equals 1 at x = 0.
